  <title>Release notes from gepa</title>
  <updated>2026-03-16T10:06:14Z</updated>
  <entry>
    <id>tag:github.com,2008:Repository/1032419366/v0.1.1</id>
    <updated>2026-03-16T10:15:46Z</updated>
    <link rel="alternate" type="text/html" href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/releases/tag/v0.1.1"/>
    <title>v0.1.1</title>
    <content type="html"><h3>Highlights</h3>
<p><strong>Candidate Tree Visualization</strong> — GEPA now generates an interactive HTML lineage tree of all candidates explored during optimization. Nodes are color-coded by role (best, Pareto front, seed) with hover previews and click-to-pin tooltips for reading full prompt text. The tree is automatically logged to WandB and MLflow at each step — no configuration needed. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/256" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/256/hovercard">#256</a>)</p>
<p><strong>gskill: Automated Skill Learning for Coding Agents</strong> — A new <code>gepa.gskill</code> module that uses <code>optimize_anything</code> to automatically discover and learn repository-specific skills for coding agents, powered by SWE-smith and Docker. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/213" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/213/hovercard">#213</a>)</p>
<p><strong>Top-K Pareto Candidate Selector</strong> — New <code>candidate_selection_strategy="top_k_pareto"</code> limits parent selection to the top-K candidates by aggregate score (default K=5), focusing mutation effort on the most promising programs. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/246" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/246/hovercard">#246</a>)</p>
<h3>Improvements</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Parallel evaluation on by default</strong> — <code>EngineConfig</code> now defaults to <code>parallel=True</code> with <code>max_workers=os.cpu_count()</code>. No configuration changes needed. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/240" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/240/hovercard">#240</a>)</li>
<li><strong>Atomic state saves</strong> — Checkpoints now use temp-file + <code>os.replace()</code>, preventing corruption on interrupted runs. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/242" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/242/hovercard">#242</a>)</li>
<li><strong>JSON run artifacts</strong> — <code>run_log.json</code> and <code>candidates.json</code> are now written to the run directory after each state save, enabling post-hoc analysis without extra code. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/245" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/245/hovercard">#245</a>)</li>
<li><strong>Cleaner experiment tracking</strong> — WandB/MLflow logging overhauled: engine config logged at start, best/seed prompts in summary, scalar metrics begin at step 0. (<a href="https://github.com/gepa-ai/gepa/pull/243" data-hovercard-type="pull_request" data-hovercard-url="/gepa-ai/
